symptoms.symptom_name: Painful feeding / feeding refusal (cries and pulls off when sucking)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sucking increases middle-ear pressure and pain, causing refusal — a classic driver in infants.
No intake is the mechanism of the fluid deficit.
Open communication between oral and nasal cavities allows fluid to escape through the nose.
Regurgitation with forced feeding raises aspiration risk in a cleft infant.
Fever accompanies suppurative otitis media.
Fever with lethargy and mottling in a young infant must trigger exclusion of SBI.

symptoms.symptom_name: Reduced urine output — no wet nappy for ~14 hours

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Oliguria/anuria is a key marker of significant dehydration in infants.

symptoms.symptom_name: Lethargy / floppiness with weak hoarse cry

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reduced responsiveness accompanies significant fluid deficit.
Altered responsiveness is a high-risk (red) feature demanding urgent assessment.
Impaired peripheral perfusion raises concern for evolving shock/sepsis; video-observable so admissible in telehealth, unverified.
A classic infant dehydration sign; also argues against raised intracranial pressure/meningitis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absent tears and tacky/dry mucous membranes

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Dry mucosa and absent tears are recognised hydration signs; partly caregiver-observable.
Prolonged capillary refill indicates impaired perfusion from volume depletion — clinician-measured, sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Tachycardia (HR 165) with low-normal BP (85/50) and tachypnoea (RR 45)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Compensatory tachycardia for volume depletion — objective clinician measurement, sealed; the AI infers compromise from lethargy and reduced output, not a measured value.

symptoms.symptom_name: Bilateral bulging erythematous tympanic membranes with purulent effusion (right)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Otoscopic bulging erythematous TMs with effusion confirm acute suppurative otitis media — obtainable only by in-person otoscopy.
No focal crackles/wheeze lowers the probability of established aspiration pneumonia — clinician auscultation, sealed.
Absence of GI losses points away from gastroenteritis; the deficit is from failure of intake plus fever.
Maintained oxygen saturation lowers the probability of significant pneumonia/hypoxia — clinician pulse oximetry, sealed.
Cleft palate causes Eustachian tube dysfunction, strongly predisposing to recurrent otitis media.
Palatal defect predisposes to nasal regurgitation and aspiration.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Together these confirm at least moderate dehydration requiring supervised rehydration and urgent assessment.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Serious-infection concern in a young infant
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Fever with lethargy and mottling keeps serious bacterial infection on the table until an in-person assessment clears it.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person paediatric assessment with otoscopy and hydration/perfusion examination
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms otitis media, grades dehydration and screens for serious bacterial infection — none achievable via telehealth.
A common occult source of fever in infants; should be excluded when the febrile source is uncertain.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Capillary blood glucose and electrolytes (if IV access/rehydration required)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Guides safe rehydration and detects hyponatraemia/hypernatraemia in a dehydrated infant.
Lungs are clear and SpO2 is maintained — routine imaging is not indicated unless respiratory signs develop.
